mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary policy, which raises the cost of capital and discounts future corporate earnings more aggressively, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid rise in the unemployment rate indicates a deteriorating labor market and broader economic distress, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the significant rise in UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous sharp decline in retail sales and housing starts signifies a severe contraction in both consumer demand and investment, indicating a deep recession that will lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% MoM AND HOUST declines > 5% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer and housing weakness are recessionary signals
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances when both RSAFS and HOUST are declining sharply.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production during a recessionary period indicates a severe contraction in manufacturing and overall economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident economic indicator
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns despite significant INDPRO declines during a confirmed recessionary period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which supports corporate earnings growth and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth correlates with market performance
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 60d returns consistently during periods of sustained GDPC1 growth (e.g., 3 consecutive quarters of positive growth).

mechanism_narrative: High and persistent consumer price inflation erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, while also potentially prompting tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances when CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Significant job losses combined with declining retail sales indicate a severe and broad-based demand shock and labor market weakness, which will lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100K MoM AND RSAFS declines > 0.5% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Job losses and weak sales signal recession
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances when both PAYEMS and RSAFS are declining significantly.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained, sharp decline in housing starts during a period of economic stress signals a deep and prolonged downturn in the housing market and broader economy, negatively impacting future economic activity and equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% MoM for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns despite sustained, sharp declines in HOUST during a confirmed housing market bust.
